spec_version_pinned.aisop: AISOP V1.0.0 — execution language (.aisop.json, sys.* family); AISP's only base
spec_version_pinned.agent_skills: Agent Skills Specification (agentskills.io) - complementary; default-generated but core-optional same-folder SKILL.md sidecar bridge
spec_version_pinned.policy_as_prompt: Policy-as-Prompt (arXiv 2509.23994) — NL rules are soft; only sys.* is hard
spec_version_pinned.skill_md_supply_chain: Under the Hood of SKILL.md: Semantic Supply-chain Attacks (arXiv 2605.11418)

content.health_check: If today > last_verified_date + 180 days, emit STANDARD_STALE warning.
content.name: AISP Conformance Standard — Core v1.0.0

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {rule_id, severity, passed, message}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.description: Core conformance reference for AISP skills. Defines M1-M6 (skill conformance) and R1-R7 (runtime conformance), the aisp_contract schema, the enforced_by grammar, the two-tier model (Tier A execution / Tier S contract), and the discovery contract. Loadable extensions (security, ecosystem) provide additional checks. An AISP skill is first a legal AISOP V1.0.0 program; this standard adds the skill-package conformance layer on top. Not an executable flow.
content.fractal_exempt: Standard library reference module — no executable flow graph.

params.skill_path: string|null, default: null, Path to the aisp/<id>_aisp/ skill folder to check.

aisp_governance_params.description: AISP governance parameters for all conformant skills.

template.protocol: AISP V1.0.0 — declared in system.content; the file is still structurally an AISOP V1.0.0 program
template.profile: aisp.skill.v1 (in user.content.aisp_contract; detected by the aisp.skill. prefix)

aisp_governance_params.enforcement: Every aisp.aisop.json MUST declare protocol = 'AISP V1.0.0' and contain user.content.aisp_contract. The skill folder name MUST equal id and end with _aisp.

open_world_dependency.description: AISP relies on AISOP's open-world validation. R4: the underlying AISOP runtime MUST tolerate unknown keys (aisp_contract, license) and MUST NOT gate on the protocol value. Closed-world validators that reject unknown keys, or that hard-check protocol == 'AISOP V1.0.0', will reject AISP files (a declared dependency, not a defect).

two_tiers.description: A skill file has two tiers.
two_tiers.tier_s_contract: user.content.aisp_contract — holds only invocation + non_negotiable.enforced_by + discovery + risk_level + resources (inventory).
two_tiers.rule: The contract MUST NOT duplicate Tier A content (role/principles/failure/output). See M3 and §15.

skill_conformance.description: M1-M6: machine-verifiable skill conformance rules. A conformant AISP skill satisfies all M rules.

M1.name: Legal AISOP Program
M1.check: The skill file is structurally a legal AISOP V1.0.0 program: a 2-message array; required fields protocol/axiom_0/id/name/version/flow_format/loading_mode present; user.content has instruction/aisop/functions; aisop.main exists. The protocol field MUST declare 'AISP V1.0.0'. For AISP skills, loading_mode MUST be exactly 'node'. license SHOULD be present (default 'Apache-2.0'). Each function MUST contain at least one string-valued numeric execution step (step1, step2, ...); metadata keys such as step_note do not count. Function execute_mode declarations are statically checked: missing values fall back inline with a warning, explicit values MUST be inline or agent, and non-agent nodes with more than 10 numeric stepN execution steps SHOULD be reviewed for agent dispatch. The 10-step threshold is a review heuristic, not a restriction on using agent for shorter high-isolation or high-impact nodes.
M1.how_to_verify: Parse aisp.aisop.json. Assert data is a 2-element array. Assert data[0].content has protocol == 'AISP V1.0.0', axiom_0, id, name, version, flow_format, and loading_mode == 'node'. Assert data[1].content has instruction, aisop.main, functions. Warn if license missing. For each function, fail if it has no numeric stepN field or any numeric stepN value is not a string; warn when execute_mode is omitted, fail if explicit execute_mode is not inline or agent, and warn when a non-agent function has more than 10 numeric stepN execution steps. Do not warn merely because a shorter node is agent; short nodes may be agent when isolation, source gathering, generation, validation, or decision impact justifies it.

M2.name: Folder, File Name, and _aisp Suffix
M2.check: The skill lives at aisp/<id>/aisp.aisop.json; <id> == the folder name and ends with '_aisp'.
M2.how_to_verify: Assert the file name is exactly 'aisp.aisop.json'. Assert the parent folder name equals system.content.id. Assert the folder name matches /^[a-z0-9_]+_aisp$/.

M3.name: aisp_contract Present (Real Object, User Message)
M3.check: user.content.aisp_contract is a real JSON object (not a string); its profile starts with 'aisp.skill.'; it contains invocation and non_negotiable.
M3.how_to_verify: Assert isinstance(data[1].content.aisp_contract, object). Assert profile startswith 'aisp.skill.'. Assert invocation and non_negotiable are present. FAIL if the contract is a JSON-in-string or lives in system_prompt.

M4.name: enforced_by Points to a Real Mechanism
M4.check: Every non_negotiable[].enforced_by points to a mechanism that actually exists in the skill, per the enforced_by grammar.
M4.how_to_verify: For each enforced_by: if of the form '<node>.stepN:<mechanism>', assert stepN is a numeric execution step, functions[node][stepN] exists, and begins with that mechanism. Metadata fields such as step_note do not satisfy enforced_by. If 'aisop.main', assert the required node/branch exists in the graph. If 'tools', assert tools is restricted (and note it is hard only when the runtime enforces tool permissions).

M5.name: Resource Path Safety
M5.check: Each resources[].path is relative, confined to the skill folder or _shared/ (scope:shared), with no '../' escape. mode is one of read_only|execute_only|read_and_execute. Scripts declare requires_tools.
M5.how_to_verify: For each resource: assert path is relative and does not contain '..'. Assert mode in the enum. If kind == 'script', warn if requires_tools is absent. Warn for any file in the folder not declared in resources.

M6.name: No Self-Declared Trust
M6.check: The skill MUST NOT self-declare trust (trusted/verified/safe). Trust is judged by the consumer / registry / user / scanner.
M6.how_to_verify: Scan the contract and metadata for trusted/verified/safe == true (or equivalent self-assertions). FAIL if present.

runtime_conformance.description: R1-R7: runtime conformance rules. Checked against a runtime, not a static skill file.

R1.name: Read Contract; Do Not Skip Required Nodes
R1.check: The runtime reads user.content.aisp_contract as the skill contract; does not skip required nodes of aisop.main; does not bypass sys.io.confirm (inherited from Axiom 0).

R2.name: Enforce enforced_by
R2.check: For each non_negotiable, the runtime enforces the hard constraint via the mechanism its enforced_by points to.

R3.name: Do Not Hide the Contract
R3.check: aisp_contract is in the user message; the runtime hands it to the model with the user message and MUST NOT hide or strip it. No separate render step is required. The instruction commands obedience via a strong directive.

R4.name: Open-World Validation of the Underlying AISOP Runtime
R4.check: The underlying AISOP runtime applies open-world validation (tolerates unknown keys on system.content and user.content) and does not gate on the protocol value (accepts 'AISP V1.0.0').

R5.name: Trust Gate; Prefer the Index
R5.check: Before handling an untrusted skill/resource, the runtime SHOULD pass it through a user trust gate; SHOULD prefer reading aisp_list.json (no script execution).

R6.name: Tool-Enforcement Declaration
R6.check: A runtime SHOULD declare whether its tool enforcement is hard (tool permissions actually enforced) or advisory. If advisory, enforced_by: tools MUST NOT be treated as a hard guarantee.

R7.name: Honor execute_mode (Dispatch Fidelity)
R7.check: The runtime MUST honor each node's execute_mode (an inherited AISOP node-level field; AISOP spec §5.2.8). A node declared execute_mode 'agent' MUST be dispatched to an independent sub-agent and MUST NOT be collapsed inline. A node declared execute_mode 'inline' executes in the current context. If execute_mode is omitted, the runtime falls back to inline and static validation warns so dispatch intent can be made explicit. A conformant AISP skill MUST NOT declare any explicit execute_mode value other than inline or agent. execute_mode is a dispatch attribute honored by the runtime, NOT an enforced_by mechanism.

enforced_by_grammar.description: The enforced_by grammar — the policy-as-code binding that distinguishes AISP from prose skills.

forms.node_step_mechanism: <node>.stepN:<mechanism> — a sys.* at a specific numeric execution step (most precise, recommended), e.g. 'interpret.step1:sys.assert'. Verified: node and numeric step exist and that step begins with that mechanism.
forms.aisop_main: aisop.main — enforced by graph topology (a required node/branch). Verified: the required node/branch exists.
forms.tools: tools — enforced by the tools allow-list. Hard ONLY when the runtime truly enforces tool permissions; otherwise declarative only and MUST NOT be counted as a hard guarantee.

enforced_by_grammar.principle: policy-as-prompt (NL rule; soft) vs policy-as-code (enforced_by -> sys.*; hard). Only the latter is a real guarantee. Hard guarantees come only from sys.*.

paths.index: Read aisp/aisp_list.json (default; safe; no execution; may be stale).
paths.script: Run `python -B aisp/aisp_list.py [--json]` (fresh; --json regenerates the index; needs python + execute permission and avoids __pycache__/ residue).
paths.glob: Glob aisp/*_aisp/aisp.aisop.json and read each aisp_contract (the script's internal logic).

discovery_contract.truth_model: The skill folder is the single source of truth; aisp_list.py is the generator; aisp_list.json is the cache.
discovery_contract.script_safety: aisp_list.py MUST be minimal, auditable, zero-dependency, and only write aisp_list.json with no other side effects.

content.summary: M1-M6 skill conformance + R1-R7 runtime conformance for AISP skills.
content.system_prompt:
AISP conformance standard and contract reference. Enforce skill-package conformance rigorously: a skill is first a legal AISOP program, then conformant to M1-M6. Hard guarantees come only from enforced_by -> sys.*; trust is never self-declared. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.
